name: Full Bleed
description: No persistent navigation. Scroll-driven hero-first layout. Used by portfolio landing pages.

header.nav_links: If navigation links are surfaced in the floating header, keep the visible label to the route name itself. Treat any declared hotkeys as keyboard bindings or command-palette hints rather than inline nav text.
header.note: Floating nav overlays hero. No border, transparent background.
header.button_sizing: Buttons in the header use compact sizing: py-1.5 px-3 text-sm (~32px tall). The header is a tight 52px bar — default d-interactive padding is too large here.

body.note: Full-width sections stack vertically. Each section is full viewport height or auto. Sections own their own padding. Natural document scroll, no scroll container.

guidance.shell_spacing: Full-bleed shell owns floating header offsets and section stack rhythm. Pages should not add extra shell-width wrappers unless a route contract explicitly calls for a local reading measure or content column.
guidance.hotkeys: When navigation hotkeys are declared in the essence, implement them as keyboard shortcuts or command-palette affordances. Do not append hotkey text to the floating header navigation unless the route contract explicitly asks for visible shortcut hints.

code.imports: Target-specific imports vary. Translate the internal_layout contract into your framework's floating header, route outlet, and full-bleed section stack.
code.example: FullBleedShell(root) -> FloatingHeader(brand, nav links, compact actions) + SectionStack(full-width sections with natural document scroll)
